meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Colombia tourist jewel plagued by violence
items.summary: Armed groups’ extortion and turf wars in Colombia’s Sierra Nevada threaten Indigenous communities and tourism boom.

items.title: FIFA chief Infantino defends World Cup ticket prices
items.summary: Eye-watering prices reflect demand to watch the World Cup in the US, Infantino says, amid criticism from fans.

items.title: US petrol prices 50 percent higher than before war on Iran
items.summary: The average price of petrol in the United States has reached $4.48 per gallon, according to AAA.

items.title: ‘A stress test’: US-Germany rift widens as Iran war drags on
items.summary: German official says US-Europe relationship is being tested as opposition MP says Berlin is 'not neutral'

items.title: Shopping centre fire kills at least eight people in Iran
items.summary: Shopping centre fire kills at least eight people in Iran

items.title: Australian women with alleged ISIL ties returning from Syria, minister says
items.summary: Australian Federal Police Commissioner Krissy Barrett says some individuals will be arrested and charged.

items.title: Thunder dominate LeBron James, Lakers to win Game 1
items.summary: A game-high 27 points by LeBron James were not enough for LA Lakers to upset Oklahoma City in Western Conference semis.

items.title: Syria says it dismantled Hezbollah-linked cell plotting assassinations
items.summary: Authorities say the cell was planning 'targeted assassinations against high-level government officials'.

items.title: US must not insult IRGC during the World Cup: Iran’s football chief
items.summary: Mehdi Taj says cohosts US must ensure the Iranian military and IRGC are not 'insulted' during the FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Top tennis players could boycott French Open over prize money: Sabalenka
items.summary: Women's tennis stars say players 'deserve to be paid more' in prize money at Grand Slam tournaments.

items.title: Iran war day 68: Trump talks about progress in talks; Rubio says war ‘over’
items.summary: Trump pauses US 'Project Freedom' operation in Strait of Hormuz, citing progress towards Iran agreement.

items.title: Manipur’s ‘unknown’ killers: Three years of India’s bloody ethnic conflict
items.summary: Violence in the remote state not only endures, but has mutated and deepened though assailants remain officially unnamed.

items.title: Syria’s Suwayda: A new hub for the multibillion-dollar Captagon trade?
items.summary: Suwayda has emerged as a new Captagon hub in Syria, triggering Jordanian strikes and a regional security crisis.

items.title: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi arrives in China for talks
items.summary: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has arrived in Beijing for talks with his Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi.

items.title: US military releases video of strike on alleged drug boat
items.summary: US military releases video of strike on alleged drug boat

items.title: Airlines hike fares, cut millions of seats as Iran war drives up fuel costs
items.summary: Surging jet fuel prices are prompting carriers to slash their flight schedules, casting a cloud over summer travel rush.

items.title: In Gaza, the simplest of weddings are barely affordable
items.summary: Couples marry in tents in Gaza as war, displacement, and soaring prices strip weddings of joy.

items.title: What to know about Israeli President Herzog’s trip to Central America?
items.summary: Israeli Foreign Ministry says that four-day visit to Panama and Costa Rica will deepen ties with partners in the region.

items.title: US president Trump restarts Pope feud ahead of Rubio visit to Vatican
items.summary: Donald Trump has reignited his public attacks on Pope Leo, claiming he supports Iran obtaining nuclear weapons.

items.title: Iran’s Araghchi holds talks with China’s Wang Yi in Beijing
items.summary: Iran's foreign minister meets his Chinese counterpart one week before President Donald Trump's visit to Beijing.

items.title: Romania’s pro-EU government ousted after no-confidence vote
items.summary: The pro-European Union coalition of Romanian Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan has collapsed after a vote of no confidence.

items.title: US pauses plan to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Donald Trump has said that the US military operation guiding ships through the Strait of Hormuz will be paused.

items.title: Spain agrees to let hantavirus-hit cruise ship dock in Canary Islands
items.summary: Three people have died since the outbreak in April, while two crew members on board the ship require urgent care.

items.title: Protesters rally in Louisiana and Tennessee against redistricting
items.summary: Demonstrators rallied in Louisiana and Tennessee against against redistricting.

items.title: Iran war live: Trump says Hormuz operation paused amid US, Tehran talks
items.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says ceasefire with Iran remains in place despite growing tensions in Hormuz Strait.
